summary: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Well this is nuts : The US government, citing national security authorities, has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the United States, including foreign national Anthropic employees. The net effect of this order is that we must abruptly disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all our customers to ensure compliance. Access to all other Anthropic models will not be affected. We received the directive from the government today at 5:21pm (ET). The letter did not provide specific details of its national security concern. Our understanding is that the government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or "jailbreaking" Fable 5. selection_dossier.core_question: 美国政府暂停Anthropic Fable 5和Mythos 5的出口管制指令，对普通技术人、小团队和AI工具依赖者意味着什么？
selection_dossier.why_this_topic_matters: 这是首次美国政府直接以国家安全为由暂停AI模型访问，且指令针对所有外国公民，包括Anthropic的外籍员工。事件揭示了AI工具依赖的脆弱性、平台合规风险的不可控性，以及小团队在规则变化面前的被动地位。对依赖Anthropic模型的开发者、企业用户和独立开发者具有直接冲击。
fact_summary: 2026年6月9日，Anthropic发布Claude Fable 5和Mythos 5，前者是首个广泛可用的Mythos级模型，后者是同一模型但解除部分安全限制，仅限受信任的网络安全和生物用户。
2026年6月12日，美国政府以国家安全为由，发出出口管制指令，要求暂停所有外国公民（包括Anthropic外籍员工）对Fable 5和Mythos 5的访问。Anthropic于当日17:21（ET）收到指令，随即暂停所有客户访问。
政府未提供具体国家安全关切细节，仅口头表示发现了一种绕过（越狱）Fable 5的方法。Anthropic审查后认为该方法仅能识别少量已知的次要漏洞，且其他公开模型也能发现这些漏洞。
越狱方法由研究员Pliny the Liberator公开，采用多智能体攻击、Unicode技巧和叙事框架，并泄露了Fable 5约12万字符的系统提示。
Fable 5定价为输入每百万token 10美元，输出每百万token 50美元。
其他Anthropic模型（如Opus 4.8）不受影响。

selection_dossier.old_flower_stance: 这件事暴露了AI工具依赖的致命风险：平台可以因外部指令随时切断服务，而你没有任何补偿或缓冲。普通技术人应该重新评估对单一模型的依赖，建立多模型备份策略，并关注合规风险。不要把所有鸡蛋放在一个篮子里。

research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Backlash and Blocks. On June 9, 2026, Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5. Three days later, Anthropic said the US government had ordered it to suspend access to both models for foreign nationals, including Anthropic’s own foreign national employees. Fable 5 was Anthropic’s first broadly available Mythos-class model. Mythos 5 was the same underlying model with some safeguards lifted for trusted cybersecurity and biology users. And one class of work, frontier LLM development, would be quietly degraded. If Fable’s classifiers detected one of those requests, Anthropic said the request would be handled by Opus 4.8 and users would be told. On page 13 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 system card, Anthropic said it had added interventions for requests about “frontier LLM development,” including work on pretraining pipelines, distributed training infrastructure, and ML accelerator design. The important sentence was that these safeguards would not be visible to the user.

research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack Exploits. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken. Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026, as the first publicly available model in its new Mythos class, its most capable AI to date, excelling in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision benchmarks. Researcher “Pliny the Liberator” defeats Claude Fable 5’s safety classifiers using multi-agent decomposition, Unicode tricks, and narrative framing, leaking the model’s 120,000-character system prompt along the way. When a query trips a classifier in high-risk categories cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, or model distillation Fable 5 silently hands off the request to the weaker Claude Opus 4.8, notifying the user of the fallback. Within days of release, prolific AI red-teamer Pliny the Liberator publicly announced he had bypassed Fable 5’s safety layers using a coordinated multi-agent attack strategy he called “a pack hunt.”. Beyond the technical bypasses, Pliny also leaked Fable 5’s ~120,000-character system prompt to GitHub, exposing the internal framing and safety instructions Anthropic uses to govern the model’s behavior at the base level.

research_evidence.fetched_text: Anthropic today [launched two new AI models](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5)— Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 — marking the company’s first broad release of the powerful “Mythos-class” AI capabilities it previously made available only to participating organizations in its restricted cybersecurity program, [Project Glasswing](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-says-its-most-powerful-ai-cyber-model-is-too-dangerous-to-release), which it announced two months ago. Anthropic says requests involving certain high-risk areas — including cybersecurity, biology and chemistry, and model distillation — are automatically routed to [Claude Opus 4.8,](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropics-claude-opus-4-8-is-here-with-3x-cheaper-fast-mode-and-near-mythos-level-alignment) Anthropic's previously flagship general model, instead, with users notified when that happens. Anthropic says Fable 5 is available to the general public today through its website, apps, and [API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview), but that Mythos 5 will initially only be made available to users who already have access to the older Claude Mythos Preview. The announcement follows Anthropic’s April 2025 rollout of Claude Mythos Preview through [Project Glasswing](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-says-its-most-powerful-ai-cyber-model-is-too-dangerous-to-release), a restricted program for cyber defenders, critical infrastructure providers and major software maintainers. * [Consent Preferences](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-brings-mythos-to-the-masses-with-claude-fable-5-its-most-powerful-generally-available-model-ever#).

We apologize for this disruption to our customers and are working to restore access as soon as possible. Read more Today we’re launching Claude Fable 5 : a Mythos-class 1 model that we’ve made safe for general use. Fable 5’s capabilities exceed those of any model we’ve ever made generally available. It is state-of-the-art on nearly all tested benchmarks of AI capability, showing exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, scientific research, and many other areas. The longer and more complex the task, the larger Fable 5’s lead over our other models. Releasing a model this capable comes with risks. Without safeguards, Fable 5’s capabilities in areas like cybersecurity could be misused to cause serious damage. We’ve therefore launched the model with safeguards that mean queries on some topics will instead receive a response from our next-most-capable model, Claude Opus 4.8. To release the model both safely and quickly, we’ve tuned these safeguards conservatively—they’ll sometimes catch harmless requests, though they trigger, on average, in less than 5% of sessions. With more capable models arriving in the coming months, we’re working to improve our safeguards and reduce false positives as quickly as we can. For a small group of cyberdefenders and infrastructure providers, we’re also launching Claude Mythos 5 . The net effect of this order is that we must abruptly disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all our customers to ensure compliance. Access to all other Anthropic models will not be affected. We received the directive from the government today at 5:21pm (ET). The letter did not provide specific details of its national security concern. Our understanding is that the government believes it has become aware of a method of bypassing, or "jailbreaking" Fable 5. We reviewed a demonstration of this specific technique being used to identify a small number of previously known, minor vulnerabilities. These vulnerabilities all appear relatively simple, and we have found that other publicly-available models are able to discover them as well without requiring a bypass. research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Backlash and Blocks. On June 9, 2026, Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5. Three days later, Anthropic said the US government had ordered it to suspend access to both models for foreign nationals, including Anthropic’s own foreign national employees. Fable 5 was Anthropic’s first broadly available Mythos-class model. Mythos 5 was the same underlying model with some safeguards lifted for trusted cybersecurity and biology users. And one class of work, frontier LLM development, would be quietly degraded. If Fable’s classifiers detected one of those requests, Anthropic said the request would be handled by Opus 4.8 and users would be told. On page 13 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 system card, Anthropic said it had added interventions for requests about “frontier LLM development,” including work on pretraining pipelines, distributed training infrastructure, and ML accelerator design. The important sentence was that these safeguards would not be visible to the user.

research_evidence.query: Fable 5 Mythos 5 jailbreak method security vulnerability
research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack Exploits. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken. Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026, as the first publicly available model in its new Mythos class, its most capable AI to date, excelling in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision benchmarks. Researcher “Pliny the Liberator” defeats Claude Fable 5’s safety classifiers using multi-agent decomposition, Unicode tricks, and narrative framing, leaking the model’s 120,000-character system prompt along the way. When a query trips a classifier in high-risk categories cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, or model distillation Fable 5 silently hands off the request to the weaker Claude Opus 4.8, notifying the user of the fallback. Within days of release, prolific AI red-teamer Pliny the Liberator publicly announced he had bypassed Fable 5’s safety layers using a coordinated multi-agent attack strategy he called “a pack hunt.”. Beyond the technical bypasses, Pliny also leaked Fable 5’s ~120,000-character system prompt to GitHub, exposing the internal framing and safety instructions Anthropic uses to govern the model’s behavior at the base level.

material_pack.core_question: 美国政府暂停Anthropic Fable 5和Mythos 5的出口管制指令，对普通技术人、小团队和AI工具依赖者意味着什么？
material_pack.why_this_topic_matters: 这是首次美国政府直接以国家安全为由暂停AI模型访问，且指令针对所有外国公民，包括Anthropic的外籍员工。事件揭示了AI工具依赖的脆弱性、平台合规风险的不可控性，以及小团队在规则变化面前的被动地位。对依赖Anthropic模型的开发者、企业用户和独立开发者具有直接冲击。
fact_summary: 2026年6月9日，Anthropic发布Claude Fable 5和Mythos 5，前者是首个广泛可用的Mythos级模型，后者是同一模型但解除部分安全限制，仅限受信任的网络安全和生物用户。
2026年6月12日，美国政府以国家安全为由，发出出口管制指令，要求暂停所有外国公民（包括Anthropic外籍员工）对Fable 5和Mythos 5的访问。Anthropic于当日17:21（ET）收到指令，随即暂停所有客户访问。
政府未提供具体国家安全关切细节，仅口头表示发现了一种绕过（越狱）Fable 5的方法。Anthropic审查后认为该方法仅能识别少量已知的次要漏洞，且其他公开模型也能发现这些漏洞。
越狱方法由研究员Pliny the Liberator公开，采用多智能体攻击、Unicode技巧和叙事框架，并泄露了Fable 5约12万字符的系统提示。
Fable 5定价为输入每百万token 10美元，输出每百万token 50美元。
其他Anthropic模型（如Opus 4.8）不受影响。
material_pack.persona_discussion_question: 老花：我该不该继续依赖Anthropic的模型？如果我的项目用了Fable 5或Mythos 5，现在该怎么办？迁移成本高吗？
material_pack.old_flower_stance: 这件事暴露了AI工具依赖的致命风险：平台可以因外部指令随时切断服务，而你没有任何补偿或缓冲。普通技术人应该重新评估对单一模型的依赖，建立多模型备份策略，并关注合规风险。不要把所有鸡蛋放在一个篮子里。

audience_fit.why_interested: 核心技术人关心模型依赖风险、迁移成本和替代方案；高价值商业读者关心合规风险和供应链稳定性；副业普通人关心是否影响自己的项目。
audience_fit.reader_risk: 标题可能被误解为“AI被禁”，需明确限制范围仅针对Fable 5和Mythos 5，且仅限外国公民。

mass_interest_hook.why_non_technical_people_may_click: “美国政府下令暂停AI模型”具有强烈冲突感，普通人会好奇“AI为什么会被禁”“会不会影响我用的ChatGPT”。
mass_interest_hook.story_seed: 一个开发者刚把项目迁移到Fable 5，三天后模型被禁，项目停摆。
mass_interest_hook.do_not_overhype: 不要夸大影响范围，明确限制仅针对两个模型和外国公民，其他模型不受影响。

research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Backlash and Blocks. On June 9, 2026, Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5. Three days later, Anthropic said the US government had ordered it to suspend access to both models for foreign nationals, including Anthropic’s own foreign national employees. Fable 5 was Anthropic’s first broadly available Mythos-class model. Mythos 5 was the same underlying model with some safeguards lifted for trusted cybersecurity and biology users. And one class of work, frontier LLM development, would be quietly degraded. If Fable’s classifiers detected one of those requests, Anthropic said the request would be handled by Opus 4.8 and users would be told. On page 13 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 system card, Anthropic said it had added interventions for requests about “frontier LLM development,” including work on pretraining pipelines, distributed training infrastructure, and ML accelerator design. The important sentence was that these safeguards would not be visible to the user.

research_evidence.title: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack ...
research_evidence.query: Fable 5 Mythos 5 jailbreak method security vulnerability
research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack Exploits. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken. Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026, as the first publicly available model in its new Mythos class, its most capable AI to date, excelling in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision benchmarks. Researcher “Pliny the Liberator” defeats Claude Fable 5’s safety classifiers using multi-agent decomposition, Unicode tricks, and narrative framing, leaking the model’s 120,000-character system prompt along the way. When a query trips a classifier in high-risk categories cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, or model distillation Fable 5 silently hands off the request to the weaker Claude Opus 4.8, notifying the user of the fallback. Within days of release, prolific AI red-teamer Pliny the Liberator publicly announced he had bypassed Fable 5’s safety layers using a coordinated multi-agent attack strategy he called “a pack hunt.”. Beyond the technical bypasses, Pliny also leaked Fable 5’s ~120,000-character system prompt to GitHub, exposing the internal framing and safety instructions Anthropic uses to govern the model’s behavior at the base level.

research_evidence.fetched_text: Anthropic today [launched two new AI models](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5)— Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 — marking the company’s first broad release of the powerful “Mythos-class” AI capabilities it previously made available only to participating organizations in its restricted cybersecurity program, [Project Glasswing](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-says-its-most-powerful-ai-cyber-model-is-too-dangerous-to-release), which it announced two months ago. Anthropic says requests involving certain high-risk areas — including cybersecurity, biology and chemistry, and model distillation — are automatically routed to [Claude Opus 4.8,](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropics-claude-opus-4-8-is-here-with-3x-cheaper-fast-mode-and-near-mythos-level-alignment) Anthropic's previously flagship general model, instead, with users notified when that happens. Anthropic says Fable 5 is available to the general public today through its website, apps, and [API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview), but that Mythos 5 will initially only be made available to users who already have access to the older Claude Mythos Preview. The announcement follows Anthropic’s April 2025 rollout of Claude Mythos Preview through [Project Glasswing](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-says-its-most-powerful-ai-cyber-model-is-too-dangerous-to-release), a restricted program for cyber defenders, critical infrastructure providers and major software maintainers. * [Consent Preferences](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-brings-mythos-to-the-masses-with-claude-fable-5-its-most-powerful-generally-available-model-ever#).

Read more Today we’re launching Claude Fable 5 : a Mythos-class 1 model that we’ve made safe for general use. Fable 5’s capabilities exceed those of any model we’ve ever made generally available. It is state-of-the-art on nearly all tested benchmarks of AI capability, showing exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, scientific research, and many other areas. The longer and more complex the task, the larger Fable 5’s lead over our other models. Releasing a model this capable comes with risks. Without safeguards, Fable 5’s capabilities in areas like cybersecurity could be misused to cause serious damage. We’ve therefore launched the model with safeguards that mean queries on some topics will instead receive a response from our next-most-capable model, Claude Opus 4.8. To release the model both safely and quickly, we’ve tuned these safeguards conservatively—they’ll sometimes catch harmless requests, though they trigger, on average, in less than 5% of sessions. With more capable models arriving in the coming months, we’re working to improve our safeguards and reduce false positives as quickly as we can. For a small group of cyberdefenders and infrastructure providers, we’re also launching Claude Mythos 5 . research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Backlash and Blocks. On June 9, 2026, Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5. Three days later, Anthropic said the US government had ordered it to suspend access to both models for foreign nationals, including Anthropic’s own foreign national employees. Fable 5 was Anthropic’s first broadly available Mythos-class model. Mythos 5 was the same underlying model with some safeguards lifted for trusted cybersecurity and biology users. And one class of work, frontier LLM development, would be quietly degraded. If Fable’s classifiers detected one of those requests, Anthropic said the request would be handled by Opus 4.8 and users would be told. On page 13 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 system card, Anthropic said it had added interventions for requests about “frontier LLM development,” including work on pretraining pipelines, distributed training infrastructure, and ML accelerator design. The important sentence was that these safeguards would not be visible to the user.

research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack Exploits. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken. Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026, as the first publicly available model in its new Mythos class, its most capable AI to date, excelling in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision benchmarks. Researcher “Pliny the Liberator” defeats Claude Fable 5’s safety classifiers using multi-agent decomposition, Unicode tricks, and narrative framing, leaking the model’s 120,000-character system prompt along the way. When a query trips a classifier in high-risk categories cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, or model distillation Fable 5 silently hands off the request to the weaker Claude Opus 4.8, notifying the user of the fallback. Within days of release, prolific AI red-teamer Pliny the Liberator publicly announced he had bypassed Fable 5’s safety layers using a coordinated multi-agent attack strategy he called “a pack hunt.”. Beyond the technical bypasses, Pliny also leaked Fable 5’s ~120,000-character system prompt to GitHub, exposing the internal framing and safety instructions Anthropic uses to govern the model’s behavior at the base level.

selection_dossier.core_question: 美国政府暂停Anthropic Fable 5和Mythos 5的出口管制指令，对普通技术人、小团队和AI工具依赖者意味着什么？
selection_dossier.why_this_topic_matters: 这是首次美国政府直接以国家安全为由暂停AI模型访问，且指令针对所有外国公民，包括Anthropic的外籍员工。事件揭示了AI工具依赖的脆弱性、平台合规风险的不可控性，以及小团队在规则变化面前的被动地位。对依赖Anthropic模型的开发者、企业用户和独立开发者具有直接冲击。
fact_summary: 2026年6月9日，Anthropic发布Claude Fable 5和Mythos 5，前者是首个广泛可用的Mythos级模型，后者是同一模型但解除部分安全限制，仅限受信任的网络安全和生物用户。
2026年6月12日，美国政府以国家安全为由，发出出口管制指令，要求暂停所有外国公民（包括Anthropic外籍员工）对Fable 5和Mythos 5的访问。Anthropic于当日17:21（ET）收到指令，随即暂停所有客户访问。
政府未提供具体国家安全关切细节，仅口头表示发现了一种绕过（越狱）Fable 5的方法。Anthropic审查后认为该方法仅能识别少量已知的次要漏洞，且其他公开模型也能发现这些漏洞。
越狱方法由研究员Pliny the Liberator公开，采用多智能体攻击、Unicode技巧和叙事框架，并泄露了Fable 5约12万字符的系统提示。
Fable 5定价为输入每百万token 10美元，输出每百万token 50美元。
其他Anthropic模型（如Opus 4.8）不受影响。
selection_dossier.persona_discussion_question: 老花：我该不该继续依赖Anthropic的模型？如果我的项目用了Fable 5或Mythos 5，现在该怎么办？迁移成本高吗？
selection_dossier.old_flower_stance: 这件事暴露了AI工具依赖的致命风险：平台可以因外部指令随时切断服务，而你没有任何补偿或缓冲。普通技术人应该重新评估对单一模型的依赖，建立多模型备份策略，并关注合规风险。不要把所有鸡蛋放在一个篮子里。

audience_fit.why_interested: 核心技术人关心模型依赖风险、迁移成本和替代方案；高价值商业读者关心合规风险和供应链稳定性；副业普通人关心是否影响自己的项目。
audience_fit.reader_risk: 标题可能被误解为“AI被禁”，需明确限制范围仅针对Fable 5和Mythos 5，且仅限外国公民。

mass_interest_hook.why_non_technical_people_may_click: “美国政府下令暂停AI模型”具有强烈冲突感，普通人会好奇“AI为什么会被禁”“会不会影响我用的ChatGPT”。
mass_interest_hook.story_seed: 一个开发者刚把项目迁移到Fable 5，三天后模型被禁，项目停摆。
mass_interest_hook.do_not_overhype: 不要夸大影响范围，明确限制仅针对两个模型和外国公民，其他模型不受影响。

research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack Exploits. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken. Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026, as the first publicly available model in its new Mythos class, its most capable AI to date, excelling in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision benchmarks. Researcher “Pliny the Liberator” defeats Claude Fable 5’s safety classifiers using multi-agent decomposition, Unicode tricks, and narrative framing, leaking the model’s 120,000-character system prompt along the way. When a query trips a classifier in high-risk categories cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, or model distillation Fable 5 silently hands off the request to the weaker Claude Opus 4.8, notifying the user of the fallback. Within days of release, prolific AI red-teamer Pliny the Liberator publicly announced he had bypassed Fable 5’s safety layers using a coordinated multi-agent attack strategy he called “a pack hunt.”. Beyond the technical bypasses, Pliny also leaked Fable 5’s ~120,000-character system prompt to GitHub, exposing the internal framing and safety instructions Anthropic uses to govern the model’s behavior at the base level.

Anthropic says requests involving certain high-risk areas — including cybersecurity, biology and chemistry, and model distillation — are automatically routed to [Claude Opus 4.8,](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropics-claude-opus-4-8-is-here-with-3x-cheaper-fast-mode-and-near-mythos-level-alignment) Anthropic's previously flagship general model, instead, with users notified when that happens. Anthropic says Fable 5 is available to the general public today through its website, apps, and [API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview), but that Mythos 5 will initially only be made available to users who already have access to the older Claude Mythos Preview. The announcement follows Anthropic’s April 2025 rollout of Claude Mythos Preview through [Project Glasswing](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-says-its-most-powerful-ai-cyber-model-is-too-dangerous-to-release), a restricted program for cyber defenders, critical infrastructure providers and major software maintainers. * [Consent Preferences](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-brings-mythos-to-the-masses-with-claude-fable-5-its-most-powerful-generally-available-model-ever#).

We apologize for this disruption to our customers and are working to restore access as soon as possible. Read more Today we’re launching Claude Fable 5 : a Mythos-class 1 model that we’ve made safe for general use. Fable 5’s capabilities exceed those of any model we’ve ever made generally available. It is state-of-the-art on nearly all tested benchmarks of AI capability, showing exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, scientific research, and many other areas. The longer and more complex the task, the larger Fable 5’s lead over our other models. Releasing a model this capable comes with risks. Without safeguards, Fable 5’s capabilities in areas like cybersecurity could be misused to cause serious damage. We’ve therefore launched the model with safeguards that mean queries on some topics will instead receive a response from our next-most-capable model, Claude Opus 4.8. To release the model both safely and quickly, we’ve tuned these safeguards conservatively—they’ll sometimes catch harmless requests, though they trigger, on average, in less than 5% of sessions. With more capable models arriving in the coming months, we’re working to improve our safeguards and reduce false positives as quickly as we can. For a small group of cyberdefenders and infrastructure providers, we’re also launching Claude Mythos 5 . research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Backlash and Blocks. On June 9, 2026, Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5. Three days later, Anthropic said the US government had ordered it to suspend access to both models for foreign nationals, including Anthropic’s own foreign national employees. Fable 5 was Anthropic’s first broadly available Mythos-class model. Mythos 5 was the same underlying model with some safeguards lifted for trusted cybersecurity and biology users. And one class of work, frontier LLM development, would be quietly degraded. If Fable’s classifiers detected one of those requests, Anthropic said the request would be handled by Opus 4.8 and users would be told. On page 13 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 system card, Anthropic said it had added interventions for requests about “frontier LLM development,” including work on pretraining pipelines, distributed training infrastructure, and ML accelerator design. The important sentence was that these safeguards would not be visible to the user.

research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack Exploits. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken. Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026, as the first publicly available model in its new Mythos class, its most capable AI to date, excelling in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision benchmarks. Researcher “Pliny the Liberator” defeats Claude Fable 5’s safety classifiers using multi-agent decomposition, Unicode tricks, and narrative framing, leaking the model’s 120,000-character system prompt along the way. When a query trips a classifier in high-risk categories cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, or model distillation Fable 5 silently hands off the request to the weaker Claude Opus 4.8, notifying the user of the fallback. Within days of release, prolific AI red-teamer Pliny the Liberator publicly announced he had bypassed Fable 5’s safety layers using a coordinated multi-agent attack strategy he called “a pack hunt.”. Beyond the technical bypasses, Pliny also leaked Fable 5’s ~120,000-character system prompt to GitHub, exposing the internal framing and safety instructions Anthropic uses to govern the model’s behavior at the base level.

material_pack.core_question: 美国政府暂停Anthropic Fable 5和Mythos 5的出口管制指令，对普通技术人、小团队和AI工具依赖者意味着什么？
material_pack.why_this_topic_matters: 这是首次美国政府直接以国家安全为由暂停AI模型访问，且指令针对所有外国公民，包括Anthropic的外籍员工。事件揭示了AI工具依赖的脆弱性、平台合规风险的不可控性，以及小团队在规则变化面前的被动地位。对依赖Anthropic模型的开发者、企业用户和独立开发者具有直接冲击。
fact_summary: 2026年6月9日，Anthropic发布Claude Fable 5和Mythos 5，前者是首个广泛可用的Mythos级模型，后者是同一模型但解除部分安全限制，仅限受信任的网络安全和生物用户。
2026年6月12日，美国政府以国家安全为由，发出出口管制指令，要求暂停所有外国公民（包括Anthropic外籍员工）对Fable 5和Mythos 5的访问。Anthropic于当日17:21（ET）收到指令，随即暂停所有客户访问。
政府未提供具体国家安全关切细节，仅口头表示发现了一种绕过（越狱）Fable 5的方法。Anthropic审查后认为该方法仅能识别少量已知的次要漏洞，且其他公开模型也能发现这些漏洞。
越狱方法由研究员Pliny the Liberator公开，采用多智能体攻击、Unicode技巧和叙事框架，并泄露了Fable 5约12万字符的系统提示。
Fable 5定价为输入每百万token 10美元，输出每百万token 50美元。
其他Anthropic模型（如Opus 4.8）不受影响。
material_pack.persona_discussion_question: 老花：我该不该继续依赖Anthropic的模型？如果我的项目用了Fable 5或Mythos 5，现在该怎么办？迁移成本高吗？
material_pack.old_flower_stance: 这件事暴露了AI工具依赖的致命风险：平台可以因外部指令随时切断服务，而你没有任何补偿或缓冲。普通技术人应该重新评估对单一模型的依赖，建立多模型备份策略，并关注合规风险。不要把所有鸡蛋放在一个篮子里。

audience_fit.reader_risk: 标题可能被误解为“AI被禁”，需明确限制范围仅针对Fable 5和Mythos 5，且仅限外国公民。

mass_interest_hook.story_seed: 一个开发者刚把项目迁移到Fable 5，三天后模型被禁，项目停摆。
mass_interest_hook.do_not_overhype: 不要夸大影响范围，明确限制仅针对两个模型和外国公民，其他模型不受影响。

research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Backlash and Blocks. On June 9, 2026, Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5. Three days later, Anthropic said the US government had ordered it to suspend access to both models for foreign nationals, including Anthropic’s own foreign national employees. Fable 5 was Anthropic’s first broadly available Mythos-class model. Mythos 5 was the same underlying model with some safeguards lifted for trusted cybersecurity and biology users. And one class of work, frontier LLM development, would be quietly degraded. If Fable’s classifiers detected one of those requests, Anthropic said the request would be handled by Opus 4.8 and users would be told. On page 13 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 system card, Anthropic said it had added interventions for requests about “frontier LLM development,” including work on pretraining pipelines, distributed training infrastructure, and ML accelerator design. The important sentence was that these safeguards would not be visible to the user.

research_evidence.title: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack ...
research_evidence.query: Fable 5 Mythos 5 jailbreak method security vulnerability
research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack Exploits. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken. Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026, as the first publicly available model in its new Mythos class, its most capable AI to date, excelling in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision benchmarks. Researcher “Pliny the Liberator” defeats Claude Fable 5’s safety classifiers using multi-agent decomposition, Unicode tricks, and narrative framing, leaking the model’s 120,000-character system prompt along the way. When a query trips a classifier in high-risk categories cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, or model distillation Fable 5 silently hands off the request to the weaker Claude Opus 4.8, notifying the user of the fallback. Within days of release, prolific AI red-teamer Pliny the Liberator publicly announced he had bypassed Fable 5’s safety layers using a coordinated multi-agent attack strategy he called “a pack hunt.”. Beyond the technical bypasses, Pliny also leaked Fable 5’s ~120,000-character system prompt to GitHub, exposing the internal framing and safety instructions Anthropic uses to govern the model’s behavior at the base level.

research_evidence.fetched_text: Anthropic today [launched two new AI models](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-fable-5-mythos-5)— Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 — marking the company’s first broad release of the powerful “Mythos-class” AI capabilities it previously made available only to participating organizations in its restricted cybersecurity program, [Project Glasswing](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-says-its-most-powerful-ai-cyber-model-is-too-dangerous-to-release), which it announced two months ago. Anthropic says requests involving certain high-risk areas — including cybersecurity, biology and chemistry, and model distillation — are automatically routed to [Claude Opus 4.8,](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropics-claude-opus-4-8-is-here-with-3x-cheaper-fast-mode-and-near-mythos-level-alignment) Anthropic's previously flagship general model, instead, with users notified when that happens. Anthropic says Fable 5 is available to the general public today through its website, apps, and [API](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview), but that Mythos 5 will initially only be made available to users who already have access to the older Claude Mythos Preview. The announcement follows Anthropic’s April 2025 rollout of Claude Mythos Preview through [Project Glasswing](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-says-its-most-powerful-ai-cyber-model-is-too-dangerous-to-release), a restricted program for cyber defenders, critical infrastructure providers and major software maintainers. * [Consent Preferences](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-brings-mythos-to-the-masses-with-claude-fable-5-its-most-powerful-generally-available-model-ever#).

We apologize for this disruption to our customers and are working to restore access as soon as possible. Read more Today we’re launching Claude Fable 5 : a Mythos-class 1 model that we’ve made safe for general use. Fable 5’s capabilities exceed those of any model we’ve ever made generally available. It is state-of-the-art on nearly all tested benchmarks of AI capability, showing exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, scientific research, and many other areas. The longer and more complex the task, the larger Fable 5’s lead over our other models. Releasing a model this capable comes with risks. Without safeguards, Fable 5’s capabilities in areas like cybersecurity could be misused to cause serious damage. We’ve therefore launched the model with safeguards that mean queries on some topics will instead receive a response from our next-most-capable model, Claude Opus 4.8. To release the model both safely and quickly, we’ve tuned these safeguards conservatively—they’ll sometimes catch harmless requests, though they trigger, on average, in less than 5% of sessions. With more capable models arriving in the coming months, we’re working to improve our safeguards and reduce false positives as quickly as we can. For a small group of cyberdefenders and infrastructure providers, we’re also launching Claude Mythos 5 . research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Backlash and Blocks. On June 9, 2026, Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5. Three days later, Anthropic said the US government had ordered it to suspend access to both models for foreign nationals, including Anthropic’s own foreign national employees. Fable 5 was Anthropic’s first broadly available Mythos-class model. Mythos 5 was the same underlying model with some safeguards lifted for trusted cybersecurity and biology users. And one class of work, frontier LLM development, would be quietly degraded. If Fable’s classifiers detected one of those requests, Anthropic said the request would be handled by Opus 4.8 and users would be told. On page 13 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 system card, Anthropic said it had added interventions for requests about “frontier LLM development,” including work on pretraining pipelines, distributed training infrastructure, and ML accelerator design. The important sentence was that these safeguards would not be visible to the user.

research_evidence.query: Fable 5 Mythos 5 jailbreak method security vulnerability
research_evidence.fetched_text: # Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken to Generate Stack Exploits. Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Jailbroken. Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026, as the first publicly available model in its new Mythos class, its most capable AI to date, excelling in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision benchmarks. Researcher “Pliny the Liberator” defeats Claude Fable 5’s safety classifiers using multi-agent decomposition, Unicode tricks, and narrative framing, leaking the model’s 120,000-character system prompt along the way. When a query trips a classifier in high-risk categories cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, or model distillation Fable 5 silently hands off the request to the weaker Claude Opus 4.8, notifying the user of the fallback. Within days of release, prolific AI red-teamer Pliny the Liberator publicly announced he had bypassed Fable 5’s safety layers using a coordinated multi-agent attack strategy he called “a pack hunt.”. Beyond the technical bypasses, Pliny also leaked Fable 5’s ~120,000-character system prompt to GitHub, exposing the internal framing and safety instructions Anthropic uses to govern the model’s behavior at the base level.
